title: Project Overview
description: Read the repository overview and workspace manifest first. They explain the stroke-rehabilitation platform and establish the client, API, recovery workflows, and quality checks explored in this tour.

title: Client Startup and Routing
description: The client entry point mounts the application, and the routing tree makes public, protected, recovery, and clinician screens reachable. Together they provide the browser-facing map before examining individual workflows.
languageLesson: React applications mount one root component and use declarative routes to select screen trees from the browser URL.

title: Authentication and Recovery Workflows
description: Authentication context supplies session-aware state to protected routes, while Axios configuration is the shared HTTP boundary. The SMART-goal and tracking screens show how a participant moves through a focused recovery workflow.

title: Conversational Support
description: The chatbot coordinates conversation state, message submission, goal context, and assistant responses. Its message and sidebar components form a stateful client feature that connects directly to the server's chat and safety path.

title: Express API and Risk Escalation
description: The server bootstraps middleware and mounts the API routes, with shared schemas validating requests at the boundary. Chat handling, risk calculation, and alert routes then turn conversational signals into clinician review workflows.
languageLesson: Express middleware forms a request pipeline, allowing requests to be enriched, validated, or rejected before route handlers run.

title: Persistent Recovery Data
description: The database connection and migrations reveal the durable model behind accounts, goals, check-ins, conversations, and alerts. Read the goal and alert tables with the migration to connect API operations to the data they preserve.
languageLesson: SQL migrations evolve a database in ordered, reviewable steps; tables and constraints preserve the relationships application routes rely on.

title: Tests, Evaluation, and Release Gates
description: Shared route-test utilities exercise server behavior through HTTP, while the evaluation runner checks chatbot scenarios against an approved baseline. The environment template and CI workflows document runtime needs and keep those safeguards repeatable for every change.
languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows separate triggers, jobs, and sequential steps, making evaluation gates repeatable in CI.
